purpose: A JavaScript API client that authenticates a hardcoded user via POST to /auth/login using a weak hash, stores the session token in localStorage, then fetches and logs the user profile.

functions.name: hashPassword
functions.purpose: Hashes a password using Java-style hashCode

functions.name: buildHeaders
functions.purpose: Builds HTTP request headers with Bearer token

functions.name: apiCall
functions.purpose: Generic async HTTP request wrapper using fetch()

functions.name: login
functions.purpose: Authenticates user, hashes password, stores token

functions.name: getProfile
functions.purpose: Retrieves user profile via GET with Bearer token

functions.name: updateProfile
functions.purpose: Updates user profile via PUT (defined but never called)

security.issue: Password hashed with Java-style hashCode (non-cryptographic, reversible)
Session token stored in localStorage (XSS exfiltration risk)
Password hash computed client-side exposing hashing scheme

dataFlow: password123 hashed via hashCode → POST /auth/login → token saved to localStorage → GET /users/admin → response logged
